id: detecting web attacks/insecure direct object reference (idor)
title: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
relPath: Detecting Web Attacks/Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR).md

breadcrumbs.id: detecting web attacks/insecure direct object reference (idor)
breadcrumbs.title: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)

overview: IDOR is a type of Broken Access Control vulnerability. It happens when an application exposes a reference (like id=123) to an internal object without properly checking if the requester is authorized to access it.

study.overview: IDOR is a type of Broken Access Control vulnerability. It happens when an application exposes a reference (like id=123) to an internal object without properly checking if the requester is authorized to access it.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
tips: OWASP 2021 lists Broken Access Control (including IDOR) as the 1 web vulnerability.
